description: Extensible network discovery, visualization, and analysis framework. Uses multiple protocols (SNMP, LLDP, CDP) for auto-discovery, generates interactive topology diagrams, and provides real-time device status. Python-based with web UI.

name: Akvorado
description: Flow collector, enricher and visualizer. Receives NetFlow/IPFIX and sFlow, enriches with SNMP interface names and geo data, stores in ClickHouse, and provides a built-in web UI with Sankey diagrams and time series. Docker Compose deployment. Built by Free (French ISP).

name: pmacct
description: Multi-purpose passive network monitoring toolset supporting NetFlow, IPFIX, sFlow, libpcap, BGP, BMP, RPKI, IGP, and Streaming Telemetry. Lightweight C-based collectors that can export to Kafka, RabbitMQ, databases. Widely used in ISP/carrier environments.

name: Edgeshark
description: Discover and capture container network traffic from desktop Wireshark with a single click. Containerized service with Wireshark extcap plugin. Supports Docker, containerd, and KinD. Great companion for containerlab network labs.

name: Netpicker
description: Network config backup, security/compliance testing, and job automation in one platform. Uses pytest for compliance checks. Supports multi-vendor config management with a web UI.

name: YANCCM
description: NETCONF-based network configuration and change management tool. Uses ncclient for config-diff, commit-confirm, and compliance against a Source of Truth. Supports YAML-defined multi-device configs.

name: Ixia-C
description: Open-source traffic generator based on the Open Traffic Generator API. Generates and analyzes network traffic for testing, validation, and performance benchmarking. Supports containerized deployment and integrates with Containerlab for network lab testing.

name: OpenTofu
description: Open-source fork of Terraform for declarative infrastructure management. Linux Foundation project with full Terraform compatibility, state encryption, and community-driven development. Relevant for Infrastructure as Code / Network as Code approaches.

name: flowlogs-pipeline
description: Observability tool that consumes network flow logs (NetFlow v5/v9, IPFIX, eBPF), transforms them, and exports to Loki and/or Prometheus metrics. Supports Kafka input, mathematical transformations, and network domain knowledge encapsulation for SREs and network experts.

name: srlinux-scrapli
description: SR Linux convenience functions for Scrapligo. Provides Go bindings for automating Nokia SR Linux devices via the Scrapli framework, enabling programmatic CLI interaction and configuration management.

name: srlinux-ndk-py
description: Python bindings for Nokia SR Linux NetOps Development Kit (NDK). Build custom agents that run natively on SR Linux — monitoring, automation, or integration agents with full access to the SR Linux state and config via gRPC protobufs.

name: Trishul SNMP Studio
description: Modern web-based SNMP management platform. Simulate SNMP agents, walk/parse devices with MIB resolution, send/receive traps with real-time WebSocket push, browse MIB trees, manage MIB files. Replaces Net-SNMP CLI, iReasoning MIB Browser, snmpsim in one Docker container. FastAPI + Python + Bootstrap 5.

name: vscode-containerlab
description: VS Code extension for containerlab. Auto-discovers .clab.yml topologies, tree view with color-coded lab states, context menu actions (deploy/destroy/SSH/logs), interface traffic capture via Wireshark/Edgeshark, link impairment controls, and interactive TopoViewer + Draw.io graph export. Great companion to containerlab for Nokia SR Linux and multi-vendor network labs.

name: ygotsrl
description: Go API for Nokia SR Linux Network OS generated with ygot (YANG Go Tools). Provides type-safe Go structures for SR Linux YANG models, enabling programmatic config management and validation.

name: bond
description: A Go package to simplify Nokia SR Linux NDK (NetOps Development Kit) app development. Provides abstractions for building custom agents that extend SR Linux functionality.

name: OneUptime
description: Complete open-source monitoring and observability platform. Self-hosted alternative to Datadog/PagerDuty with status pages, incident management, on-call rotation, telemetry (OpenTelemetry-native), dashboards, and alerting. Apache 2.0 license.

name: Skydive
description: Real-time network topology and protocols analyzer. Agents collect topology info and flows, forwarding to a central agent for analysis. SDN-agnostic with driver support. Stores in Elasticsearch. Supports VMs, containers, and has a unified query language.

name: ktranslate
description: Network data collection and translation system by Kentik Labs. Pulls SNMP, flow (NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX), and streaming telemetry data and pushes to multiple sinks (Kafka, Prometheus, New Relic, stdout, S3, file). Includes SNMP discovery, MIB browsing, and device walk capabilities.

name: GoBMP
description: Go-based BGP Monitoring Protocol (BMP) implementation. Full SRv6 support with BGP-LS extensions (Flex Algo), multiple publishers (Kafka, NATS, file), OpenBMP compatibility, intercept/proxy mode, and built-in profiling. Useful for BGP route monitoring and SR-MPLS visibility.

name: ciena.waveserverai
description: Ansible Collection for Ciena Waveserver Ai optical platforms. Supports NETCONF connection for automated management of Waveserver Ai transponders and muxponders. Includes resource modules for xcvrs configuration.

name: ServiceRadar
description: Zero-trust distributed network monitoring platform for infrastructure in hard-to-reach or constrained environments. Features WASM plugin system (Go/Rust), GPU-native topology engine, distributed agent/gateway/core architecture, and cloud-based alerting. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Upright
description: Self-hosted synthetic monitoring system from 37signals (Basecamp/HEY). Playwright browser probes, HTTP checks, SMTP verification, traceroute with hop-by-hop latency, multi-site support, Prometheus metrics, OpenTelemetry tracing, AlertManager integration. Rails + SQLite + Docker.

name: sig
description: Interactive grep for streaming data. Real-time search through live data streams with TUI, command re-execution for missed data, archived mode for historical search. Rust-based, fast. Great for tailing network logs and filtering in real-time.

name: aiNOC
description: AI-based network troubleshooting project using Claude Code, FastMCP, Python, Scrapli, and Containerlab. Multi-vendor (Cisco, Arista, MikroTik), multi-protocol topology with structured outputs via Genie/eAPI/REST. Demonstrates AI-assisted NOC operations with tool caching and hallucination reduction.

name: NetOpsForge
description: AI-assisted zero-code network automation platform with self-growing pack library. Aligned to Cisco AUTOCOR v2.0. Features documentation site, quick-start guides, and a visual project board. Designed for network engineers transitioning to automation.

name: Network Importer
description: Tool/library to analyze and synchronize an existing network with a Network Source of Truth (SOT). Idempotent by default, shows differences between running network and remote SOT. Integrates with Batfish, DiffSync, NetBox, and Nornir.

name: jdiff
description: Python library for examining and comparing structured data (JSON). Useful for network config drift detection, pre/post change validation, and compliance checks. Compare command outputs, API responses, or any structured data.

name: Nokia EDA
description: Nokia Event Driven Automation (EDA) documentation and examples. EDA enables intent-based, event-driven network automation for Nokia SR Linux and SR OS platforms using declarative intents and reactive workflows.

name: ciena.saos8
description: Official Ansible Collection for Ciena SAOS 8.x devices. Includes modules for running commands (saos8_command) and collecting facts (saos8_facts) via network_cli. Tested against SAOS 8.6.5.

name: cienasaos10ncc
description: Python library for interacting with Ciena SAOS 10 devices via NETCONF/YANG. Supports querying classifiers, forwarding domains, flow points, and more. Lightweight alternative to full Ansible collections.

name: GtExporter
description: YANG data-model-aware gNMI streaming telemetry exporter for Prometheus. Subscribes to OpenConfig-compliant devices and exports metrics with proper YANG path awareness, making it ideal for network observability pipelines.

name: nokia-basic-dci-lab
description: Containerlab-based DCI lab with SR Linux leaf/spine and Nokia 7750 SR OS DC gateway and P routers. Demonstrates VXLAN/EVPN fabrics interconnected via BGP-VPN WAN.

name: snmp-to-otel
description: SNMP gauge metric exporter for OpenTelemetry in C++. Polls SNMP agents at configurable intervals and forwards metrics via OTLP/HTTP. Supports custom OID-to-metric name mappings.

name: SR Linux NDK Protobufs
description: Official Nokia SR Linux NetOps Development Kit (NDK) protobuf definitions. Defines the gRPC APIs for building custom agents on SR Linux. Essential reference for NDK agent development.

name: Intent-Based Ansible Lab
description: Containerlab-based lab demonstrating intent-based network automation with Ansible and Nokia SR Linux. Shows how to use Ansible for declarative, intent-driven config management on SR Linux nodes.

name: Secure Cartography
description: SSH & SNMP-based network discovery and topology mapping tool. Crawls infrastructure via CDP/LLDP, auto-generates topology maps. v2 features async discovery, SNMP v2c/v3, AES-256 credential vault, embedded topology viewer with PyQt6 GUI, and CLI with test/discover/crawl commands.

name: Netprobe Lite
description: Self-hosted internet performance monitoring tool. Measures packet loss, latency, jitter, DNS performance, and optional bandwidth speed tests. Aggregates metrics into a health score. Docker-based with Prometheus/Grafana integration for dashboards.

name: netcfgbu
description: Async network configuration backup tool using SSH. Multi-vendor support for any NOS with monolithic config (show running-config). Git integration, config diffing, inventory from CSV/Netbox, filtering by OS/host. Python asyncio for speed.

name: network.backup
description: Ansible Validated Content for network config backup and restore. Platform-agnostic roles for backup, compare, tag, and restore operations. Supports local and remote data stores. Red Hat Community of Practice.

name: Trippy
description: Modern network diagnostic tool combining traceroute and ping with a beautiful TUI. Written in Rust, cross-platform, supports ICMP/UDP/TCP, DNS-over-TLS, GeoIP, and chart/table views. Great alternative to mtr.

name: NetVisor
description: Automatic network topology discovery and interactive visualization. Scans networks, identifies hosts/services, generates interactive topology maps. Docker-based server with distributed daemon agents. Self-hosted.

name: NetClaw
description: CCIE-level AI network engineering agent built on OpenClaw with 82 skills and 37 MCP server backends. Covers Cisco, Juniper, Arista, F5, Meraki, SD-WAN, NSO, CML, ContainerLab, Grafana, Prometheus, BGP/OSPF, GitHub config-as-code, and ITSM gating with immutable audit trails.

name: OpenROADM MSA
description: Open ROADM Multi-Source Agreement YANG models and specifications for disaggregated optical transport (DWDM/ROADM). Defines interoperable device, network, and service models for optical equipment. v7.0 released. Relevant for understanding vendor-agnostic optical transport management.

name: Atlas
description: Full-stack containerized tool to scan, analyze, and visualize network infrastructure. Go-powered scanner with FastAPI backend and React frontend. Auto-discovers Docker containers, local/neighboring hosts, open ports, OS fingerprints, and renders interactive network graphs in real-time.

name: Nerdlog
description: Fast, remote-first, multi-host TUI log viewer with timeline histogram and no central server. SSH-based log querying from multiple machines simultaneously with pattern filtering and time range selection. Handles 1GB+ log files efficiently. No setup or central infrastructure needed.

name: LogTide
description: Open-source, self-hosted log management platform. Privacy-first alternative to Datadog and ELK. Lightweight, easy to deploy, designed for teams that want log aggregation without sending data to third parties.

name: NORFAB
description: Network Automations Fabric — service-oriented platform for extreme network automation. Run on laptop or server, centralized or distributed. Built-in Nornir integration for show commands, config management, CLI/REST/Python APIs. Successor to Salt-Nornir, uses ZeroMQ broker pattern for distributed workers.

name: NetAlertX
description: Centralized network visibility and continuous asset discovery. Self-hosted Docker app that monitors devices, detects changes, identifies Shadow IT, and maintains a real-time network inventory (NSoT). Supports distributed multi-site scanning, Home Assistant integration, and compliance workflows.

name: clio
description: OpenTelemetry to gNMI bridge by OpenConfig. Converts OpenTelemetry data into gNMI format, enabling integration between OTel observability pipelines and gNMI-based network telemetry systems.

name: Octelium
description: Next-gen FOSS self-hosted unified zero trust secure access platform. Operates as remote access VPN, ZTNA platform, API/AI/MCP gateway, PaaS, and ngrok-alternative. Apache-2.0/AGPL licensed.

name: network-mcp
description: AI-powered multi-vendor network automation MCP server with 261 purpose-built tools for Arista EOS, Cisco IOS-XE/NX-OS, and Juniper JunOS. Works with Claude, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client. Supports RBAC, audit logging, OpenTelemetry, mTLS, and full troubleshooting/validation/change management workflows.

name: Kubenet
description: Nokia-backed community project using Kubernetes as a network automation engine for managing physical, virtual, or containerized NOS devices (Nokia SR Linux and multi-vendor). Declarative, event-driven, continuously reconciling — treats network devices like Kubernetes resources.

name: Validity
description: NetBox plugin for writing compliance 'auto-tests' for network devices. Define compliance tests and Validity checks device state or running configuration against them. Integrates with Nautobot-style workflows; reports which devices pass or fail compliance checks.

name: ansible-navigator
description: Text-based TUI (terminal UI) for Ansible. Browse playbooks, collections, inventories, and documentation interactively. Supports execution environments (containers with ansible-core pre-loaded) and integrates with podman/docker. Dramatically improves the Ansible workflow compared to raw CLI.

name: BGPalerter
description: Self-configuring BGP and RPKI monitoring tool. Monitors prefix visibility loss, hijacks, RPKI invalid announcements, ROA misconfigurations, unexpected AS path changes, and more — all without connecting to your own network (uses public BGP data sources). Alerts via Slack, email, Kafka, and others.

name: VyManager
description: Modern web UI for centralized configuration, deployment, and monitoring of multi-site VyOS routers. Supports all active VyOS versions including rolling releases. Open beta; useful for teams managing VyOS-based infrastructure or lab environments.

name: pyribbon
description: Python module for Sonus/Ribbon SBC REST API. Query data, create/update resources, perform reboots, and do configuration backups/restores via Ribbon's REST interface. Practical tool for automating Ribbon SBC management from Python scripts.

name: nautobot-ai-ops
description: Nautobot AI Plugin integrating Large Language Models (LLM) with Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Langchain. Enables AI-powered network operations via Nautobot with dynamic prompts and MCP middleware. Early-stage but actively developed for LLM-driven network automation.

name: nokia-sros-maintenance-scripts
description: Python automation scripts for Nokia SROS maintenance tasks: bulk filesystem cleanup and card/flash status reporting with HTML dashboards. Written by Equinix engineers to handle common day-2 SROS maintenance operations at scale.

name: ciena.waveserver5
description: Official Ansible collection for Ciena Waveserver 5 optical transport appliances. Provides NETCONF-based modules for AAA, port, PTP, system, and transceiver configuration and operational data retrieval. Supports Ansible 2.12+.

name: nokia-sros-automation
description: Demo collection of Python scripts showing Nokia SR OS automation via all major interfaces: pySROS (YANG data model access), MD-CLI, config get/set, operational state retrieval, and YANG tree printing. Useful reference for bootstrapping Nokia automation workflows.

name: OpenNTI
description: Containerized open network telemetry collector and visualization stack. Collects data from devices via CLI/Netconf, JTI streaming telemetry, and Statsd. Comes pre-configured with Grafana dashboards and can run on server, laptop, or the device itself via Docker.

name: nautobot-app-circuit-maintenance
description: Nautobot plugin for tracking circuit maintenance windows from ISP/carrier notification emails (Zayo, Verizon, Lumen, Cogent, etc.). No support for Nokia/Ciena/Ribbon equipment vendors — those are gear vendors, not maintenance notification providers.
notes: Vendor tags were wrong — this parses carrier NOC emails, not equipment vendor management. Low relevance for TJ (internal utility transport, not buying carrier circuits from these providers).

name: nautobot-app-ssot
description: Nautobot Single Source of Truth (SSoT) app. Synchronizes data between Nautobot and external systems including SolarWinds, LibreNMS, Infoblox, IPFabric, ServiceNow, Arista CloudVision, Cisco DNA Center, Device42, and more. Built on the DiffSync library for clean bidirectional data sync jobs.

name: ttp_templates
description: Community library of TTP (Template Text Parser) templates for parsing network device CLI output into structured data. Covers Nokia SR OS, Cisco IOS/XR/NX-OS, Juniper, Arista EOS, and other platforms. Installable via pip (ttp-templates). Companion to the TTP library for multi-vendor text parsing without regex boilerplate.

name: Nokia Validated Designs
description: Official Nokia Validated Designs (NVDs) repository with deployable digital twins using containerized Nokia SR Linux and Containerlab. Covers multi-vendor and Nokia-centric network architectures across market segments including transport, data center, and edge. Each design includes containerlab topologies, configs, and documentation for lab recreation.

name: Nokia OSSMediator
description: Go-based mediator that periodically collects Performance Management (PM) and Fault Management (FM) data from Nokia DAC (Digital Automation Cloud) APIs and forwards it to third-party NMS systems like OpenNMS or Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. Includes Grafana dashboards for PM/FM visualization. Useful for integrating Nokia-managed networks into existing OSS/observability stacks.

name: freeRtr
description: Free open-source router OS process written in Java. Speaks a comprehensive set of routing protocols (OSPF, IS-IS, BGP, EIGRP, RIFT, Babel) and supports MPLS, Segment Routing (SR-TE, SRv6), RSVP-TE, BIER, PCEP, EVPN/VPLS, MACSec/IPSec, VXLAN, and more. Exportable forwarding tables to external dataplanes (P4, DPDK). Useful as a transport network protocol lab/emulation platform and PCE testbed.

name: pydantify
description: CLI tool that transforms YANG models into Pydantic datastructures, serializable as RESTCONF payloads. Bridges YANG/NETCONF data modeling with modern Python type-safe workflows. Supports trimming to specific YANG subtree paths, JSON schema output, and config/state filtering. Useful for building type-safe Python automation against NETCONF/RESTCONF APIs on Nokia SROS, SR Linux, and other YANG-managed devices.

name: Orb Agent (NetBox Labs)
description: Lightweight network observability and discovery agent from NetBox Labs, part of the NetBox Discovery solution. Pluggable backend architecture supporting SNMP discovery, network device discovery, and general network observability. Configurable via local YAML or Git-backed config; supports HashiCorp Vault for secrets management. Designed to feed discovered data back into NetBox/Nautobot-style source-of-truth systems.

name: ARouteServer
description: Python tool to automatically build feature-rich, policy-driven configurations for BGP route servers (BIRD v1/v2, OpenBGPD). Reads simple YAML policy definitions and enriches them with IRR/RPKI/PeeringDB data to produce validated, tested configs. Includes Docker-based live test framework and a playground IXP environment. Relevant for utilities running BGP peering or inter-domain routing infrastructure.

name: nautobot-app-bgp-models
description: Official Nautobot app extending core models with BGP-specific data — AS numbers, BGP peerings, peer groups, peer endpoints, and routing policies. Enables modeling and tracking BGP peering sessions whether or not the peer device is in Nautobot. Sponsored initially by Riot Games.

name: privacynet
description: Privacy-preserving network telemetry toolkit — IP anonymization (prefix-preserving, truncation, random), flow aggregation, and differential privacy (Laplace noise) for safely sharing NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX data with collaborators or in research contexts. Configurable low/medium/high privacy levels.

name: Scanlyne
description: Lightweight network change detection built on nmap. Runs scans, stores baseline snapshots in SQLite, and diffs subsequent scans to surface new/removed hosts and port/service changes — with triage hints for each change (e.g., common reverse shell port, unexpected DB exposure). Web UI included. Ideal for self-hosted infra audits.

name: GoFlow2
description: High-performance NetFlow v5/v9, IPFIX, and sFlow collector in Go — a maintained fork of Cloudflare's original GoFlow. Ingests flow data from routers/switches, serializes to protobuf or JSON, and ships to Kafka, files, or stdout for pipeline ingestion. Designed for horizontal scale with protocol diversity normalization into a single consistent format. Ideal for carrier/transport traffic analysis.

name: VictoriaMetrics
description: Fast, cost-effective, and scalable time series database and monitoring solution. Prometheus-compatible (drop-in replacement) with significantly better compression and query performance at scale. Supports Graphite, OpenTelemetry, InfluxDB, and Datadog ingest protocols. Used by Grammarly, Roblox, Spotify. Excellent for storing high-cardinality network telemetry (gNMI streaming, SNMP polling) without InfluxDB's licensing headaches.

name: nokia-eda-playground
description: Official Nokia EDA (Event Driven Automation) playground environment — Makefile-driven lab setup with example topologies, configs, and scripts for experimenting with Nokia's Kubernetes-native network automation platform. Pairs with nokia-eda/docs for hands-on EDA learning without a full lab deployment.

name: Trigger
description: Robust network automation toolkit in Python, originally from AOL's Network Security team. Supports SSH/Telnet/JunOS XML API, async parallel command execution, ACL/firewall policy parsing and conversion, RANCID integration, maintenance window scheduling, and encrypted credential storage. Designed for managing hundreds to thousands of devices at scale.

name: NOC Project
description: Open-source Operation Support System (OSS) designed for telecom companies, ISPs, and enterprise NOCs. Features fault management with root cause analysis and topology correlation, performance management via SNMP/CLI with long-term metrics and auto-dashboards, inventory (physical + logical), multi-protocol topology discovery, and 80+ vendor support. Scales from single-node to 300k+ device clusters.

name: ConfigDrift
description: Real-time network configuration drift detector with severity scoring. Uses NAPALM/SSH to continuously poll devices, diff running configs against golden baselines, and score changes by impact: CRITICAL (ACL/AAA/crypto), HIGH (interface/IP), MEDIUM (SNMP/NTP/logging), LOW (banners/descriptions). Stores diffs in SQLite and alerts via Slack, email, or webhook with deduplication and suppression.

name: gNMIc Operator
description: Kubernetes operator for deploying and managing gNMIc telemetry collectors at scale. Define telemetry infrastructure as Custom Resources — the operator handles StatefulSet creation, configuration generation, target distribution via bounded-load rendezvous hashing, and dynamic pipeline updates. Supports gRPC Tunnel for devices behind NAT/firewalls and TLS/mTLS with cert-manager. Enables horizontal scaling with automatic target redistribution across collectors.

name: TWSNMP FK
description: Next-generation Network Management System built with Go, Svelte, and Wails for a lightweight desktop NMS experience. Ultra-lightweight SNMP manager with real-time network maps, event logs, and gNMI telemetry support. Runs as a single binary desktop app for Windows/Linux/macOS. Good for quick NOC dashboard or monitoring a small device fleet without heavy infrastructure.

name: DiGSiViz
description: gNMI-powered real-time visualization of Network Digital Twin topologies running in Containerlab. A proof-of-concept frontend+backend app that connects to Containerlab-deployed devices via gNMI and renders live topology views in the browser. Useful for visualizing lab environments and validating telemetry pipelines before production deployment.

name: hyperglass
description: Self-hosted network looking glass that makes BGP route queries, community lookups, AS path queries, ping, and traceroute accessible via a modern web UI. Built-in Nokia SR OS support along with Arista EOS, Junos, IOS-XR, FRRouting, and more. REST API with auto-generated OpenAPI docs. Ideal for giving NOC teams and customers visibility into network routing without CLI access.

name: Netipam
description: Self-hosted IP address management (IPAM) and network visibility tool. Docker-based, easy to deploy, designed for networks from homelabs to small enterprises. Provides IP inventory, allocation tracking, and network discovery. No cloud required — pairs well with Nautobot and NetBox in a network source-of-truth stack.

name: ncdiff
description: Lightweight NETCONF diff engine (open sourced by Cisco). Compares NETCONF config states, calculates edit-config deltas, and validates YANG-modeled configurations. Standalone library usable outside pyATS. Useful for detecting config drift, verifying change impact, and building config assurance workflows against Nokia, IOS-XR, and other NETCONF-capable devices.

name: Posh-Ribbon
description: PowerShell module for controlling Ribbon SBC Edge devices via REST API. Provides cmdlets for querying state, creating/updating resources, and managing Ribbon Session Border Controllers. Complements pyribbon for teams that prefer PowerShell automation workflows. Supports Ribbon SBC in Azure deployments.

name: OpenNetworkDiagram
description: Declarative, self-hosted containerized tool for visualizing and managing network architecture diagrams. Stores topology in a JSON source of truth that can be version-controlled in Git. Includes network view with ethernet labels and a non-network host/service inventory view. Docker-based, runs in minutes. Useful for keeping network documentation alongside automation code.

name: BNG Blaster
description: Open-source network tester for routing and access protocols. Tests IS-IS, OSPF, LDP, BGP, PPPoE, IPoE/DHCP, IPTV, and L2TP at scale. Includes a traffic generator that can simulate millions of flows for QoS validation, convergence time measurement, and full-table forwarding verification. Used in production by Deutsche Telekom. Excellent for validating Nokia, Ribbon, and other carrier-grade equipment behavior before production deployment.

name: Rackula
description: Open-source drag-and-drop rack layout designer that runs entirely in the browser. Supports real device images, drag-and-drop placement, and export functionality. Self-hosted via Docker or usable offline. Useful for rack documentation, data center planning, and sharing equipment layouts with team members without proprietary software.

name: EDA Telemetry Lab
description: Reference lab showing modern telemetry architecture for Nokia EDA + SR Linux data center fabrics. Deploys a full Containerlab topology integrated with Nokia Event Driven Automation and streams telemetry to Prometheus/Grafana. Practical blueprint for setting up streaming telemetry pipelines on Nokia SR Linux deployments.

name: EDA Topo Builder
description: GUI-based topology builder for creating NetworkTopology workflows in Nokia EDA. Allows network engineers to graphically design and deploy fabric topologies that feed directly into the EDA intent engine — no YAML hand-coding required. Bridges the gap between visual design and infrastructure-as-code for Nokia EDA environments.

name: Monocle (BGPKIT)
description: Fast CLI tool for analyzing BGP data from public MRT archives (RouteViews, RIPE RIS). Written in Rust. Quickly queries historical BGP routing tables, traces prefix announcements/withdrawals, and inspects AS paths — directly from the command line with no database required. Useful for prefix-level troubleshooting and route origin verification on a transport network.

name: mcp-server-sros
description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Nokia SR OS devices. Provides AI-accessible tools for viewing operational state and changing configuration using Nokia's pySROS Python SDK and FastMCP. Bridges LLM-driven automation directly to SROS.

name: edit4config
description: Python tool for parsing and editing Nokia SROS and Cisco IOS style configs (parent/child with space indent) with regex support. Supports add/delete/replace/search operations on hierarchical config blocks — handy for config diffs and automated changes without NETCONF.

name: mapgl
description: Grafana panel plugin that renders an interactive network node graph and geomap. Visualize network topology — nodes, links, and geographic overlays — directly in Grafana dashboards. Useful for building live topology views of transport networks alongside existing telemetry panels.

name: RadOps
description: AI-powered multi-agent platform for NetDevOps and security automation. Uses Supervisor-Worker architecture with 3-tier cognitive memory (working, ephemeral, core knowledge). Supports multi-step operations across infrastructure with guardrailed orchestration. Integrates with OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, and other LLM providers.

name: NetDriver
description: Network device CLI automation via HTTP RESTful API. Connects to network devices and executes CLI commands through a simple REST interface, simplifying integration with network automation workflows and reducing SSH/CLI complexity for developers.

name: Triglav
description: gNMI/JTI-based streaming telemetry collector for Juniper transport routers (ACX, MX, PTX). Uses Telegraf for gNMI collection (SSL/gRPC) and ships metrics to VictoriaMetrics. Supports per-platform Telegraf instances to handle model differences across Junos/EVO versions.

name: pydantic-srlinux
description: Experimental Pydantic model library auto-generated from Nokia SR Linux YANG schemas. Provides strongly-typed Python classes for SR Linux config payloads (interfaces, ACL, BFD, BGP, network-instance, etc.). Pairs with pydantify for YANG-to-Pydantic code generation. Ideal for building type-safe automation against SR Linux NETCONF/JSON-RPC APIs.

name: wsl-containerlab
description: Pre-configured WSL2 Linux distribution purpose-built for Containerlab network labbing on Windows. Bundles Docker, Containerlab, and all required tooling with one-click WSL install. Supports Windows 10/11, VM-based NOSes on Windows 11. Eliminates manual Linux environment setup for Windows-based network engineers.

name: opennms-api-wrapper
description: Unofficial Python 3 client for the OpenNMS REST API (Horizon 35+ / Meridian 2024.3+). Covers all v1 and v2 REST endpoints with JSON-only interface (no XML required). Single dependency (requests), TypedDict schemas for write payloads, typed exception hierarchy, and a pagination helper. Enables Python automation and integration against OpenNMS-based network monitoring deployments.

name: xNMS
description: Active eNMS fork tailored for carrier/telecom networks. Adds VPLS E2E, EVPN-VXLAN, and MPLS link service templates on top of eNMS's workflow automation, Git-backed config backup, Netmiko/NAPALM validation, and Ansible/REST/Python script services. Imports topology from NetBox, LibreNMS, or OpenNMS.

name: Ciena YANG Modules
description: Official Ciena YANG data models for NETCONF/RESTCONF automation of Ciena platforms (SAOS, WaveServer, etc.). Actively maintained — last updated November 2025. Essential reference for building YANG-driven automation against legacy Ciena Carrier Ethernet gear.

name: Checkmate
description: Self-hosted open-source uptime and infrastructure monitoring platform. Tracks server hardware (CPU, memory, disk, network), HTTP/TCP uptime, response times, and incidents in real-time with a clean React dashboard. Supports 300+ monitors per instance with low resource overhead. Alternative to UptimeRobot/Uptime Kuma with richer infra metrics.

name: NetGauze
description: High-performance Rust libraries and daemons for network protocol parsing and telemetry collection. Supports BGP-4, MP-BGP (IPv4/IPv6, MPLS VPN, EVPN, BGP-LS), BMP v3/v4, IPFIX/NetFlow (with Nokia-specific enterprise IEs), and UDP-notif. Network telemetry collector that ingests multi-source data. Type-safe protocol parsing with invalid-state-unrepresentable guarantees via Rust's type system.

name: Keep
description: Open-source AIOps and alert management platform. Provides a single pane of glass for all alerts with deduplication, correlation, enrichment, and filtering. Supports bi-directional integrations with Grafana, Prometheus, PagerDuty, LibreNMS, OpenNMS, Zabbix and 100+ other tools. Workflow engine similar to GitHub Actions for monitoring automation. Self-hostable via Docker.

name: Rousette
description: Full RFC 8040 RESTCONF server built on top of sysrepo YANG datastore. Supports XML and JSON encoding, YANG module library, data retrieval/edits, RPC/action execution, NETCONF notification streams, YANG Patch (RFC 8072), and NACM access control (RFC 8341). Complements Netopeer2/sysrepo stack for exposing YANG-modeled data via RESTCONF HTTP API. Active development from CESNET/CzechLight.

name: cawk
description: Multi-supplier network configuration checker built purely on gawk, gmake, and gm4 — no databases, no additional languages. Validates network device configs across multiple vendors including Nokia SROS. Designed for portability and simplicity: define custom assessments (audits) and run them against any vendor's configs. Useful for config compliance checking and drift detection across multi-vendor transport environments.

name: Kubeshark
description: Cluster-wide network observability for Kubernetes. Captures L4 packets and L7 API calls (HTTP, gRPC, Redis, Kafka, DNS) using eBPF with full Kubernetes context. Decrypts TLS traffic without managing keys. Provides real-time service dependency mapping, traffic replay, and AI-powered incident investigation via MCP integration. Zero instrumentation — deploy and immediately capture.

name: Stratum
description: Open source silicon-independent switch operating system for software-defined networks. Exposes P4Runtime and OpenConfig/gNMI interfaces, enabling programmable forwarding and vendor-agnostic management. Supports Barefoot Tofino, Broadcom Tomahawk, and the bmv2 software switch. Relevant for whitebox switch deployments and gNMI-based telemetry collection.

name: nautobot-app-device-onboarding
description: Official Nautobot app for automated device onboarding. Uses netmiko and NAPALM to SSH into a device and auto-populate Nautobot with hostname, platform, manufacturer, serial number, device type, management interface, interfaces, VLANs, and more. Reduces device import from hours to seconds — just provide an IP and location. Directly relevant for onboarding Nokia, Ribbon, and Ciena gear into Nautobot as part of the modernization push.

name: multivendor-evpn-lab
description: Containerlab-based multivendor EVPN lab topology. Spins up Nokia SROS (route reflectors), Nokia SR Linux (leaf), Arista cEOS, and Juniper vQFX in a two-tier Clos fabric with L2 EVPN and OSPF underlay. Useful for testing Nokia SROS/SR Linux interop in realistic transport scenarios, validating configs pre-deployment, or learning EVPN across vendors.

name: yang-to-ngsi-ld
description: Research tool that translates YANG data models to NGSI-LD format to enable Network Digital Twin (NDT) solutions. Uses pyang plugins to auto-generate NGSI-LD schemas from YANG modules, enabling gNMI/NETCONF telemetry data to feed into digital twin context brokers. Relevant for model-driven telemetry pipelines and anyone exploring automated YANG-to-context-broker workflows for transport network management.

name: netbox-config-diff
description: NetBox plugin that finds diffs between rendered device configurations in NetBox and the actual running configs on devices, then pushes and applies them. Supports Cisco IOS-XE/IOS-XR/NX-OS, Juniper JunOS, and Arista EOS via Scrapli. Bridges the gap between NetBox config rendering (introduced in v3.5) and actual device state — very useful for drift detection and automated remediation workflows.

name: libiec61850
description: Open-source (GPLv3) C library implementing IEC 61850 client and server protocols including MMS, GOOSE, and Sampled Values. Highly portable — runs on Linux, Windows, MacOS, and embedded systems. Includes Python bindings (experimental). Relevant for electric utility networks with SEL ICON or substation automation equipment that speak IEC 61850.

name: twampy
description: Official Nokia Python implementation of TWAMP (RFC 5357) and STAMP (RFC 8762, 8972) for active network measurement. Measures latency, jitter, and packet loss with no external dependencies. Supports DSCP/TOS marking for QoS validation, jumbo frames, IMIX traffic patterns, and dual-stack IPv4/IPv6. Originally developed to validate Nokia SR OS and SR Linux TWAMP implementations — directly applicable to transport network SLA testing and performance baselining.

name: moler
description: Nokia's Python library for building automated network tests. Provides reusable 'bricks' for command-as-object automation with built-in parsing, event observers, and background execution. Supports SSH/serial connections and can model network device CLIs as typed command objects. Useful for building robust network automation test frameworks on top of Nokia or multi-vendor environments.

name: napalm-ciena-saos
description: NAPALM community driver for Ciena SAOS devices. Enables get_facts, get_config, save_config, and get_virtual_switch via SSH. Integrates Ciena gear into the broader NAPALM automation ecosystem alongside Nornir and Nautobot.

name: nautobot-app-nornir
description: Official Nautobot app providing a Nornir ORM-based inventory and credential manager. Acts as the foundation shim for other Nautobot automation apps (Golden Config, Network Importer). Enables Nornir Jobs to run directly from Nautobot.

name: cnaas-nms
description: Campus Network-as-a-Service NMS by SUNET. Open source software to automate campus LAN management: zero-touch provisioning, common change automation, firmware upgrades, and multi-vendor support. REST API with Swagger UI, Docker-ready.

name: mermin
description: Kubernetes-native network observability tool that uses eBPF to auto-instrument network traffic and export it as Flow Traces via OpenTelemetry. Provides deep visibility into cluster communications without code changes.

name: netbox-branching
description: Official NetBox Labs plugin implementing git-like branching functionality for NetBox. Allows creating isolated branches of the network source of truth for safe what-if planning and staged changes before merging to main.

name: netbox-librenms-plugin
description: NetBox plugin that syncs data bidirectionally between LibreNMS and NetBox. Keeps your network source of truth and monitoring platform in sync — devices, interfaces, IPs, and more.

name: LMTE
description: LLM-driven WAN Traffic Engineering framework (INFOCOM 2026). Uses language models to reason about WAN TE problems. Combines domain-aware prompts, multimodal embeddings, and a lightweight planning head. First LM-driven TE framework that supports effective multimodal alignment and efficient configuration generation. Research artifact with Python/PyTorch implementation and real-world dataset support (GEANT, etc.).

name: Fluere
description: Cross-platform Rust tool that captures network packets in pcap format and converts them to NetFlow v5 data. Supports live capture, offline analysis, AWS Traffic Mirroring flow logs, and includes a Terminal User Interface (TUI) for real-time feedback. Useful for generating flow data from taps or span ports on transport network segments without dedicated flow hardware.

name: Statime
description: A full Rust implementation of IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol (PTP). Supports ordinary clock, boundary clock, and transparent clock roles. Actively maintained by the Pendulum Project (which also maintains ntpd-rs). Highly relevant for telecom transport networks where PTP is used for frequency and phase synchronization across Nokia, Ribbon, and Ciena equipment.

name: Infix
description: Immutable, YANG-native Linux NOS that turns any ARM or x86 device (Raspberry Pi to enterprise switches) into a manageable network appliance. Auto-generates CLI from YANG models, supports NETCONF and RESTCONF natively, uses atomic upgrades with instant rollback, and has GNS3 integration for digital twin labs. Ideal for edge network appliances, lab testing, or building low-cost network elements compatible with Nokia/Ribbon vendor management stacks.

name: RustNet
description: Cross-platform terminal UI network monitor written in Rust. Tracks TCP/UDP/ICMP/ARP connections in real-time with deep packet inspection (DPI) to identify HTTP, TLS/SNI, DNS, SSH, QUIC, SNMP, DHCP, and NetBIOS protocols. Detects TCP retransmissions, out-of-order packets, per-connection analytics, process identification, GeoIP, and reverse DNS. Useful for live visibility on network equipment management hosts and automation servers — surfaces rogue connections and protocol anomalies fast.

name: FossFLOW
description: Open-source isometric infrastructure diagramming tool for creating beautiful, hand-crafted infrastructure diagrams. Web-based editor with drag-and-drop components for servers, routers, switches, racks, and cloud icons in a 3D isometric style. Great for documenting Nokia/Ribbon/Ciena transport topology, data center layouts, and WAN diagrams in a visually compelling format beyond standard Visio/draw.io flat diagrams.

name: NetGraf
description: End-to-end ML-driven network performance monitoring system from ESnet. Integrates packet, flow, and device-level data from multiple FOSS monitoring tools into a unified dashboard. Uses Ansible for automated deployment and machine learning libraries to learn baseline performance and detect real-time anomalies. Designed for research/carrier network environments with multi-source data aggregation.

name: ntopng
description: High-speed web-based network traffic analysis and flow monitoring tool. Supports deep packet inspection (DPI) via nDPI, NetFlow/IPFIX/sFlow ingestion, real-time host/flow/protocol dashboards, behavioral threat detection, historical traffic analysis, and Grafana/InfluxDB integration. GPL version is self-hostable with no license fee. Widely used in carrier/ISP environments for traffic visibility.

name: nDPI
description: Open Deep Packet Inspection library from ntop, used under the hood in ntopng, Wireshark, and dozens of commercial appliances. Identifies 500+ application protocols (including BGP, MPLS, gRPC, OpenFlow, Syslog, SNMP, and telecom-specific flows) in traffic streams with no need for port-based classification. Embeddable C library. Highly relevant for traffic characterization on transport network spans and SPAN/mirror ports.

name: gNSI
description: gRPC Network Security Interface — OpenConfig specification for security infrastructure services on network devices. Covers authorization, accounting, certificate management, console access management, and SSH key/cert management via gRPC. Complements gNMI/gNOI by adding a standardized security operations layer. Supported by Nokia SR Linux and other OpenConfig-compliant platforms.

name: gNPSI
description: gRPC Network Packet Sampling Interface — OpenConfig proposal to replace sFlow/NetFlow with a gRPC-based streaming packet sampling API. Addresses UDP transport loss issues, adds encryption/authentication, and uses dial-in model compatible with security requirements. Streams sampled packets over authenticated gRPC channels alongside gNMI telemetry. Early-stage but signals the future direction of packet sampling for OpenConfig platforms (Nokia, Arista, etc.).

name: gRIBI
description: gRPC Routing Information Base Interface — OpenConfig protocol for programmatically injecting routing entries into a network device's RIB from an external controller. Enables SDN-style route programming via gRPC with transactional semantics, election/redundancy support, and AFT (Abstract Forwarding Table) entry types. Used for traffic engineering and centralized path computation on Nokia, Arista, and Juniper platforms.

name: rdb-tool
description: Python tool for manipulating Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories (SEL) relay database files (.RDB). Iterates across large folder structures of RDB files for automated processing, logic counting, section extraction, and interoperability with protection relay configurations.

name: RibbonEdgePsRest
description: PowerShell module for controlling Ribbon SBC Edge via REST API. Query transformation tables, system info, create/modify/delete resources, perform reboots and configuration backups/restores at scale across multiple SBCs.

name: NetBox MCP Server
description: Official Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for NetBox by NetBox Labs. Read-only interaction with NetBox data via LLMs supporting MCP. Query objects, get details by ID, retrieve changelogs. Enables AI-assisted network infrastructure queries against your source of truth.

name: Thola
description: Go-based tool for monitoring network devices, primarily via SNMP. Features an identify mode (auto-detects vendor/model), read mode (interfaces, CPU, memory, hardware health), and check mode (Nagios/Icinga/Zabbix-compatible monitoring plugin output). Supports a wide range of vendors out of the box. Can run as a standalone CLI or REST API server for centralized polling.

name: OpenNMS
description: Enterprise-grade open-source network management platform. Supports SNMP, JSON, WinRM, XML, SQL, JMX, SFTP, FTP, JDBC, HTTP, HTTPS, VMware, WS-Management, Prometheus, and more — no third-party plugins required. Includes event/alarm management, topology visualization, flow analysis (NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX), and Grafana integration. Available as Horizon (community) and Meridian (stable LTS) editions. Scales to tens of thousands of devices.

name: Coroot
description: Open-source observability and APM tool with AI-powered Root Cause Analysis. Combines metrics, logs, traces, continuous profiling, and SLO-based alerting with predefined dashboards and inspections. Uses eBPF for zero-instrumentation service map discovery. Self-hosted alternative to Datadog/New Relic with significantly less setup overhead. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: SEL_RDB
description: Python toolkit for working with SEL (Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories) .rdb relay database files. Parses and manipulates configuration data for SEL protective relays used in power system protection. Enables automated processing and programmatic access to relay settings — useful for utilities managing SEL relay fleets.

name: GoBGP
description: Full BGP implementation in Go. Library and CLI for building BGP-based applications — route servers, looking glasses, SDN controllers, and custom routing daemons. Supports all major AFI/SAFI families, policy engine, gRPC API, MRT dump, BMP, RPKI, and Flowspec. Used as the BGP engine in several SDN projects.

name: Beszel
description: Lightweight self-hosted server monitoring hub. Historical data, Docker container stats, alerting, multi-server dashboard. Simple agent-based architecture with minimal resource footprint. Great for monitoring lab and automation infrastructure.

name: Jalapeno
description: Cloud-native infrastructure platform for network services built on BMP, GoBGP, Kafka, ArangoDB, and Kubernetes. Collects BGP-LS, BGP peering, and telemetry data to build a real-time network topology graph database. Enables SR-TE/SRv6 path computation and SDN applications for transport networks.

name: Grafana Alloy
description: Open-source OpenTelemetry Collector distribution from Grafana Labs with built-in Prometheus pipelines. Unified agent for collecting and shipping metrics, logs, traces, and profiles. Replaces the deprecated Grafana Agent. Supports SNMP, syslog, and OTLP receivers — making it a natural bridge between network device telemetry and the Grafana LGTM stack.

name: DeepFlow
description: eBPF-powered zero-code observability platform. Automatically captures application, network, and infrastructure metrics/traces/logs without code instrumentation. Features distributed tracing across processes, threads, and network interfaces with full-stack correlation. Integrates with Grafana and supports Prometheus/OpenTelemetry. CNCF sandbox project.

name: ttl
description: Fast, modern traceroute with real-time TUI, per-hop stats, ASN/geo lookup, ECMP detection, and MPLS label parsing. A better mtr. Written in Rust with ratatui. Especially useful for transport engineers working with MPLS networks — shows label stacks inline.

name: Bootz
description: OpenConfig network device bootstrap APIs and services. Defines a structured data format and gRPC service for taking devices from factory state to production — covering boot config, security config, and dynamic config as separate declarative units. Supports both dial-out and dial-in bootstrap workflows.

name: srl-prometheus-exporter
description: Prometheus exporter that runs as a native NDK app on Nokia SR Linux. Exports metrics directly from the NOS without external polling — ideal for tight Grafana/Prometheus integration with Nokia DC fabric nodes.

name: Sloggo
description: Minimal RFC 5424 syslog collector and viewer powered by DuckDB. Runs as a single, resource-friendly process with a clean web UI for browsing and searching syslog messages. No external database required — DuckDB handles storage and analytical queries in-process. Docker-ready. Ideal for collecting syslog from network devices (Nokia, Ciena, Ribbon) without deploying a full ELK/Loki stack.

name: Homelable
description: Self-hosted homelab infrastructure visualizer with interactive network diagrams and live status monitoring. TypeScript-based, generates visual maps of infrastructure with real-time health checks. Docker-friendly. Useful for building quick visual overviews of lab environments, automation servers, and test infrastructure without heavyweight NMS tools.

name: gNMI MCP Server (Nokia SR OS)
description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for managing Nokia SR OS devices via gNMI (gRPC) from Claude Desktop or any MCP-compatible AI client. Provides gNMI Get (config + state), gNMI Set (update/replace/delete), MD-CLI commands via Nokia gNMI CLI extension, and Capabilities discovery. Supports multi-device management with runtime credentials (no passwords in config files) and clean JSON-IETF encoding. More capable than pySROS-based alternatives — uses native gNMI protocol directly.

name: SROS gRPC Services
description: Official Nokia repository providing an interactive gRPC shell (grpc_shell) and Python service libraries for Nokia SR OS gNMI and RibApi operations. Includes proto files for all Nokia-supported gRPC services, generated Python bindings, and a CLI shell for interactive gNMI Get/Set and RibApi RPC calls. Essential building block for custom gRPC-based automation against Nokia SR OS routers.

name: SigNoz
description: Open-source OpenTelemetry-native observability platform with logs, traces, and metrics in a single application. Self-hosted alternative to Datadog and New Relic. Built on ClickHouse for fast queries at scale. Features distributed tracing, log management with pipeline processing, metrics with PromQL-compatible queries, alerting, and dashboards. Supports SNMP and syslog receivers via OTel Collector for network device monitoring. Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Parseable
description: Observability datalake built in Rust, designed as a lightweight alternative to Elasticsearch and Splunk for log ingestion and analysis. Uses Apache Parquet columnar storage for efficient querying and compression. Self-hosted via Docker with minimal resource footprint. REST API for log ingestion, SQL-based querying, and alerting. Good fit for collecting syslog/structured logs from network devices without the overhead of a full ELK stack.

name: selprotopy
description: Python bindings for the SEL Protocol suite (Fast Meter, Fast Message, Fast Operate) used by Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories protective relays and IEDs. Enables programmatic communication with SEL devices for data collection, monitoring, and control. Directly relevant for utility environments with SEL ICON and RTAC equipment. Community-developed, Apache 2.0 licensed.

name: Nokia Network as Code (Python SDK)
description: Official Nokia Python SDK for their Network as Code platform. Provides programmatic access to network capabilities including quality-of-service management, network-based location services, and analytics via Nokia NaC APIs. Useful for exploring Nokia programmable network capabilities and integrating Nokia infrastructure into automation workflows.

name: NAPALM SR Linux
description: Community NAPALM driver for Nokia SR Linux OS. Uses gNMI and JSON-RPC to provide the unified NAPALM API (get_facts, get_interfaces, config management) against SR Linux devices. Leverages Nokia SRL YANG models. Validated on SRL OS 21.3.1+. Complements the existing NAPALM-SROS driver for 7750/SR OS.

name: Diode
description: Data ingestion service for NetBox from NetBox Labs. Provides a gRPC-based ingestion API and reconciliation engine that accepts network discovery data (from scripts, SNMP walks, or agents) and intelligently creates/updates NetBox objects. Includes Python and Go SDKs. Bridges the gap between network discovery tools and NetBox as source of truth — directly relevant to Nautobot/NetBox automation pipelines.

name: TransportPCE
description: OpenDaylight-based open-source controller for optical transport networks. Implements path computation (PCE), topology management, service provisioning, and optical line management (OLM) using OpenROADM MSA YANG models. Supports OpenROADM device models v1.2.1, v2.2.1, and v7.x. Provisions end-to-end services via OpenConfig 2.0 and integrates with OpenROADM hardware for disaggregated optical transport.

name: MDT Dialout Collector
description: Multi-vendor Model-Driven Telemetry (MDT) collector using gRPC dialout mode. Accepts streaming telemetry from network devices (Cisco IOS-XR/NX-OS, Nokia SROS/SRL, Juniper) via gRPC dialout, decodes protobuf/JSON payloads, and forwards to Kafka or file output. Supports OpenConfig and vendor-specific YANG-push telemetry. Written in C++ for high-throughput production deployments.

name: NetContext MCP
description: AI-powered network automation through Model Context Protocol. Provides SSH-based command execution on network devices (Cisco IOS/IOS-XE, HP/Aruba ProCurve, Ubiquiti UniFi, generic Linux/SSH) via MCP servers. Supports legacy SSH algorithms for older equipment, batch operations, device-specific pagination handling, and vendor-specific error detection. Enables natural language network management through Claude Desktop or any MCP-compatible AI client.

name: Nokia NSP Integration Bootstrap
description: Official Nokia starter project for Network Service Platform (NSP) API integration. Provides sample code and client libraries for calling NSP REST APIs (nsp.developer.nokia.com). Useful for bootstrapping automation against Nokia NSP for service provisioning, inventory, fault management, and network planning. BSD-3-Clause licensed.

name: srpls
description: Language Server Protocol (LSP) implementation for Nokia SR Linux and SR OS configuration files. Provides IDE-level autocompletion, diagnostics, and navigation for Nokia device configs in VS Code and other LSP-compatible editors. Brand new from the srl-labs team.

name: evpn-ninja
description: Network engineer toolkit for VXLAN/EVPN fabric design with multi-vendor config generation. Calculate VNI, MTU, RD/RT values and generate EVPN configurations for Arista, Cisco, Juniper, Nokia, and 14+ more vendors. Useful for transport engineers designing EVPN fabrics.

name: Flowpipe
description: Cloud scripting engine for automation and workflow orchestration. Pipelines are defined as HCL code — version-controlled, composable, and shareable. Steps include HTTP calls, human input approval, messaging, queries, and sub-pipeline execution. Triggers via webhooks, queries, or schedules. Library mods for AWS, Azure, GCP, GitHub, Jira, Slack, Teams, and more. Useful for building network change approval workflows and runbook automation.

name: BGPKIT Parser
description: High-performance MRT/BGP/BMP message parser in Rust. Comparable speed to C-based bgpdump/bgpreader with ergonomic Rust API. Handles remote or local files, bzip2/gz compressed data out of the box. Supports FlowSpec NLRI (RFC 5575) parsing. Useful for analyzing RouteViews/RIPE RIS archives, building BGP analytics pipelines, and transport network route analysis.

name: nokia-config-visualizer
description: Web-based Nokia device configuration visualizer. Parses text-format Nokia configs, extracts interface/port/connected-device info, and generates Mermaid.js diagrams. Grafana Diagram panel compatible for real-time monitoring integration. TypeScript-based.

name: NetworkOps Platform
description: AI-powered network automation platform using NetBox and Model Context Protocol (MCP). 178 tools for multi-vendor infrastructure management, self-healing agents, drift detection, and a real-time web dashboard. Python/MIT licensed.

name: Kula
description: Lightweight, self-contained Linux server monitoring tool written in Go. Single binary with zero dependencies — includes both a terminal TUI and web UI. Real-time CPU, memory, disk, network, and process monitoring. Created Feb 2026 and already at 758 stars. Perfect for monitoring network automation servers, jump boxes, or any Linux host without deploying a full monitoring stack.

name: Network Sketcher
description: AI-powered network diagram generator by Cisco Open. Generates network configuration diagrams in PowerPoint and manages configuration data in Excel. Ver 3.0+ includes an Online (Flask web service) edition with LLM integration for AI-native network design creation and updates, plus an Offline desktop GUI/CLI edition. Useful for rapid network documentation, topology diagrams, and config management without Visio.

name: Ansible WebUI
description: Lightweight, self-hosted web UI for running Ansible playbooks locally. Install via pip or Docker and get a browser-based interface for managing and executing playbooks, inventories, and scheduled jobs — without the overhead of AWX/Tower. Supports cron-style job scheduling, multiple database backends (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL), and unprivileged container images. Great for small teams or lab environments that want a quick Ansible frontend.

name: NetPulse
description: High-performance SSH-to-API bridge that transforms CLI interactions on network switches and Linux servers into a structured RESTful API. Features distributed controller-worker architecture with Redis-backed task dispatch, self-healing SSH session pooling, multi-vendor CLI support (Cisco, Arista, HP Comware), SFTP sync, sudo elevation, and AI-agent-ready context via llms.txt.

name: ICS Incident Response Framework
description: Open-source incident response automation for ICS/SCADA environments. Playbook-driven engine for detecting and responding to threats in OT systems including unauthorized PLC logic downloads, historian tampering, and SIS compromise. Features forensic preservation, operator alerting (Teams), STIX/MISP integration, NIST SP 800-53 compliance mapping, and interactive CLI.
